metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2924
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Apache (aka. KIAA1107, gene BTBD8) co-localizes with SYT1/AP2A1/DNM1 and binds specifically to AP2 on clathrin-coated vesicles (Fig. 1-3).

Figure 5, S5: Mouse cortical neurons were transduced with a lentiviral vector driving the expression of control shRNA or KIAA1107-knockdown-shRNA at 12 DIV. After 5 days, KIAA1107 became undetectable and transmission electron microscopy revealed a strong reduction in synaptic vesicle density and moderate increased size of endosome-like structures (presumably to compensate for clathrin-mediated endocytosis defects). These ultrastructural effects were rescued by coinfection with EGFP-KIAA1107.

Figure 6: "To uncover whether SV recycling is impaired in KIAA1107-KD synapses, we imaged shRNActr- and shRNA#2-infected neurons by electron microscopy upon action potential (AP) firing in the presence of soluble horseradish peroxidase (HRP) to visualize the formation of endocytic intermediates. ... The increased endosomal size observed in silenced synapses both under basal conditions (Figures 5B, S6A, and S6B) and after stimulation (Figure S6C) is compatible with a longer retention of SVs at endosomal level, strongly suggesting an impaired clathrin-mediated SV reformation from endosomal structures."

"In summary, these functional data provide strong evidence that KIAA1107 plays an important role both in CME at the plasma membrane and in the reformation of SVs by clathrin coats budding from endosomes. The kinetics of SV reformation, especially under conditions of low-frequency activity when CME is the predominant pathway for SV recycling, is affected by KIAA1107 silencing. Based on these data, we named the KIAA1107 protein APache, for AP2-interacting clathrin-endocytosis protein.". Experimental description: species used in Figure 5:
"Mouse cortical neurons were transduced with a lentiviral vector driving the expression of shRNA#2 or shRNActr at 12 DIV."

on the protein identifier:
"Here, we characterize the highly conserved AP2-interacting clathrin-endocytosis protein APache (NP_001007575.2) and investigate its physiological role in neuronal development and synaptic function."

NCBI accession NP_001007575 matches uniprot accession D3YUB6 (gene names Btbd8, Gm16115, Kiaa1107)
